title: MainWindow function summary grid: add DataGrid above editor
description:
Goal:
Create the function summary data grid above the editor control in LogicFriday1.Views.MainWindow.

Definition of Done:
- A DataGrid appears above the equation/editor area.
- The grid has these columns in order: Function, Inputs, Outputs, True, False, DC, PI, Gates.
- The grid is wired to MainWindowViewModel data and can later be populated from real function model data.
- The implementation uses the Avalonia DataGrid package and includes the required DataGrid theme.
- Automated or manual verification notes are added when implemented.

Original source references:
- Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0040e929_FUN_0040e929.c creates the original Win32 SysListView32 summary grid with command/control id 0x69 and inserts columns using LVM_INSERTCOLUMN (0x101b).
- 0040e929_FUN_0040e929.c sets the original column headers in this order: Function, Inputs, Outputs, True, False, DC, PI, Gates. It also sets initial placeholder row text to "<none>".
- Decompiled/strings.tsv contains the relevant header strings: 0044b8f4 "Gates", 0044b904 "False", 0044b914 "Outputs", 0044b91c "Inputs", 0044b924 "Function". The remaining short labels are referenced by pointers in 0040e929_FUN_0040e929.c: DAT_0044b90c ("True"), DAT_0044b900 ("DC"), and DAT_0044b8fc ("PI").
- Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004195f3_FUN_004195f3.c shows related list-view setup for truth-table/editor columns, confirming the app uses list-view grids above edit/detail controls.

Avalonia DataGrid notes:
- The Avalonia DataGrid is provided by the separate Avalonia.Controls.DataGrid NuGet package.
- The DataGrid theme must be included, e.g. StyleInclude Source="avares://Avalonia.Controls.DataGrid/Themes/Fluent.xaml"; without this style include the grid may render blank.
- Reference: Roland König, "Das DataGrid von Avalonia", section "Die ersten Schritte", https://www.rolandk.de/blog/2022/10/08/das-datagrid-von-avalonia/

Implementation notes:
- Added Avalonia.Controls.DataGrid 12.0.0 to LogicFriday1/LogicFriday1.csproj. NuGet does not currently provide 12.0.3 for this package; 12.0.0 is the available Avalonia 12 DataGrid package.
- Added the DataGrid Fluent theme include to LogicFriday1/App.axaml.
- Added LogicFriday1/Models/FunctionSummaryRow.cs as an immutable record with Function, Inputs, Outputs, True, False, DC, PI, and Gates properties.
- Added FunctionSummaries and SelectedFunctionSummary to MainWindowViewModel.
- Seeded FunctionSummaries with a "<none>" placeholder row, matching the original list-view initialization.
- Added a read-only DataGrid above the equation editor in LogicFriday1/Views/MainWindow.axaml with the original column order.

Verification:
- dotnet build .\LogicFriday1.slnx succeeds with 0 warnings and 0 errors.

Status:
- Implemented locally and awaiting user verification. Do not close until confirmed.

title: MainWindow Truthtable UI: implement Menu: Truthtable > Submit (Enter)
description:
Goal:
Implement the Truth Table Submit action in LogicFriday1.Views.MainWindow.

Definition of Done:
- The context-menu Submit item and Truthtable > Submit (Enter) execute the same behavior.
- Enter on the truth-table grid submits, matching the original ListView return behavior.
- Submit creates/registers a new function entry instead of overwriting the first existing summary row.
- The function summary row matches the original format: multi-output names as first-last output (for example F0-F2), input count, output count, per-output True counts, per-output False counts, per-output DC counts, Unminimized, and Not mapped.
- Submit commits the visible truth-table rows into the current function state, regenerates the displayed equation text with the Entered by truthtable: prefix, refreshes the function summary row, and closes truth-table edit mode.
- Selecting a committed function with truth-table data shows the reduced read-only truth table on the left and the generated logic equation on the right.
- Build verification is recorded before closing this issue.

Original Source Analysis:
- Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c maps Truth Table Submit Enter command 0xe5 to the same submit block as command 0x8002.
- Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004198c3_FUN_004198c3.c sends command 0x8002 on NM_RETURN from the editable truth-table ListView.
- Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0041e0c1_FUN_0041e0c1.c commits the temporary truth-table data, calls FUN_0042093b(..., 1, "Entered by truthtable:"), clears model state at +0x23c, and returns the generated equation pointer.
- Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00421b02_FUN_00421b02.c copies row count, input/output counts and names, output arrays, true counts, and don't-care counts from the temporary truth-table block into the function model.
- Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00415a02_FUN_00415a02.c state 9 clears active edit-mode flags and transitions back to normal result/view state.
- Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0040eb81_FUN_0040eb81.c adds/registers a new function-list item. For multiple outputs it formats the Function column as first output name through last output name (for example F0-F2), not F0, F1, F2. It fills Inputs and Outputs as counts, writes per-output True/False/DC text into columns 3-5, and initializes columns 6 and 7 as Unminimized and Not mapped.
- Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0040f38f_FUN_0040f38f.c updates an existing function-list row using the same column formatting rules; it writes Unminimized when the model has no minimized data and Not mapped when no gate diagram exists.
- Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00417d61_FUN_00417d61.c rebuilds the read-only truth-table display from a selected function model. It builds a row cache and, unless Show All Rows is enabled, omits rows where every output is 0. It also handles all-true/all-false compression flags.
- Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00419da2_FUN_00419da2.c supplies text for that read-only truth-table display from the row cache created by FUN_00417d61, including the reduced-table rows.
- Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0040fb46_FUN_0040fb46.c detects single function selection changes and signals the main window to refresh the selected function display.
- Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0042093b_FUN_0042093b.c dispatches equation generation by selected format; for default SOP format it calls FUN_004209b2.
- Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004209b2_FUN_004209b2.c writes/generated equation text and uses the non-empty prefix passed by FUN_0041e0c1. The required displayed text therefore starts with Entered by truthtable: followed by generated equations.
- Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004219f6_FUN_004219f6.c appends the generated equation text into the model display buffer.

Notes:
Cancel discards editor state. Submit commits the current truth-table rows, regenerates equation text, refreshes UI summary/display, and then closes editor mode. Multiple submitted truth tables must remain separate function entries. Function selection should show a reduced read-only truth table on the left and equation text on the right, matching the original two-pane display.
notes: Additional source/model note: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0041c785_FUN_0041c785.c initializes stored display labels including Entered:, Entered by truthtable:, Minimized:, Unminimized Product of Sums:, and Minimized Product of Sums:. Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0041e1a4_FUN_0041e1a4.c writes Factored: when loading the factored equation output, and Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00421093_FUN_00421093.c writes Minimized Product of Sums: when emitting POS text. The Avalonia model should represent committed functions as concrete function types (truth table, logic equation, gate diagram) instead of an enum/source flag so each type can own its available display artifacts and labels.

title: MainWindow View Help UI: implement menu behaviors
description:
Goal:
Implement the appropriate behavior and controls that should execute when the user clicks the View and Help menu items in LogicFriday1.Views.MainWindow.

Definition of Done:
- View and Help menu items in MainWindow.axaml have implemented click/command behavior where currently missing.
- Existing About behavior is checked against the original application and adjusted if needed.
- Behavior matches the original Logic Friday application as closely as practical.
- Automated or manual verification notes are added when implemented.

Controls to analyze:
- View > Unminimized
- View > Minimized
- Help > Help Contents
- Help > About Logic Friday...

Notes:
There are decompiled sources and mapping files in .\Decompiled\ and .\Decompiled\logicfriday_decompiled_functions\ that should be used for reference. Avoid using Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled.c directly because it is too large; prefer the per-function files and mapping/reference files.

Analysis findings:
Current UI context:
- LogicFriday1/Views/MainWindow.axaml currently defines View > _Unminimized and View > _Minimized with no Click/Command handler. Help > _Help Contents also has no handler. Help > _About Logic Friday... is wired to AboutLogicFriday_OnClick.

Original View menu behavior:
- View > Unminimized is Win32 command id 0xa2 in Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c (FUN_004016d2, main window procedure at address 0x004016d2). On WM_INITMENUPOPUP/menu refresh, command 0xa2 is enabled when exactly one function is selected and no busy/edit mode is active; it is radio-checked when selected function field +0x240 / param_1[0x90] is 0. On click, the command sets DAT_0046c1ac[0x90] = 0, then refreshes the selected function/output display through FUN_0040bdc3, FUN_0042093b, FUN_00415a02, starts FUN_0040c130 for output refresh, and rebuilds dependent views via FUN_00417c56, FUN_00417d61, and FUN_00439d71. Intended behavior: switch the selected function display to the unminimized representation, then refresh the displayed function text/truth-table/list UI.
- View > Minimized is Win32 command id 0xa3 in Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c (FUN_004016d2 at 0x004016d2). On menu refresh, command 0xa3 is enabled only when exactly one function is selected, no busy/edit mode is active, and selected function field +0x23c indicates a minimized representation is available. It is radio-checked when selected function field +0x240 / param_1[0x90] is nonzero. On click, the command sets DAT_0046c1ac[0x90] = 1 and runs the same refresh path as Unminimized. Intended behavior: switch the selected function display to the minimized representation, then refresh the displayed function text/truth-table/list UI.
- The View labels are present in the original resource strings as Decompiled/strings.tsv addresses 0x00471362 "&Unminimized" and 0x0047138a "&Minimized". FUN_00417d61 in Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00417d61_FUN_00417d61.c uses param_1[0x90] (+0x240) to choose whether to populate the table/display from the unminimized truth-table arrays or from the minimized data arrays, confirming the 0xa2/0xa3 mode switch effect.

Original Help menu behavior:
- Help > Help Contents is Win32 command id 0 in Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c (FUN_004016d2 at 0x004016d2). The same behavior is also used for WM_HELP (message 0x53). It formats the help target as "%s\\lf.chm::/features.htm" and calls FUN_0043e36f(owner hwnd, target, 0, 0). FUN_0043e36f in Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0043e36f_FUN_0043e36f.c (address 0x0043e36f) lazy-loads the HTML Help control from the registry via FUN_0043e408 or falls back to hhctrl.ocx, then dispatches ordinal 0xe, i.e. HtmlHelpA. Intended behavior: open the bundled lf.chm help file to /features.htm using HTML Help with display-topic command 0.
- Help > About Logic Friday... is Win32 command id 0x68 in Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c (FUN_004016d2 at 0x004016d2). It opens DialogBoxParamA(DAT_00452914, "ABOUTDLG", owner, FUN_0040bfb7, 0). The label is present in Decompiled/strings.tsv at address 0x00471666 "&About Logic Friday...".
- The original About dialog procedure is FUN_0040bfb7 in Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0040bfb7_FUN_0040bfb7.c (address 0x0040bfb7). On WM_INITDIALOG it sets control 0x47d to "Logic Friday v. %d.%d.%d" and initializes hyperlink-style controls. OK/Cancel close the dialog. Commands 0x493 and 0x494 call FUN_004013e5, which opens the email/web targets with ShellExecuteA and shows Logic Friday error boxes if no registered mail app/browser exists. The hyperlink targets are in Decompiled/strings.tsv at 0x0045106c logic.friday@sontrak.com, 0x00451170 mailto:logic.friday@sontrak.com, 0x00451384 www.sontrak.com, and 0x00451488 http://www.sontrak.com. Intended behavior: show the About dialog with version/copyright/tool credits and clickable email/web links.

title: MainWindow Gates UI: implement menu and zoom toolbar behaviors
description:

- Related zoom toolbar buttons have matching behavior and enabled/disabled states.
- Behavior matches the original Logic Friday application as closely as practical.

Controls to analyze:
- Gates > Modify Gate Diagram
- Gates > Copy to Clipboard
- Gates > IC Package Info...
- Gates > Trace Gate Logic
- Gates > Auto Redraw Diagram
- Gates > Zoom In
- Gates > Zoom Out
- Gates > Zoom All
- Gates > Delete Selected
- Gates > Submit (Enter)
- Gates > Cancel (Esc)
- Toolbar: Zoom In, Zoom Out

Notes:
There are decompiled sources and mapping files in .\Decompiled\ and .\Decompiled\logicfriday_decompiled_functions\ that should be used for reference. Avoid using Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled.c directly because it is too large; prefer the per-function files and mapping/reference files.

Analysis findings:
- Current Avalonia UI declares the Gates menu items without click/command bindings, and the toolbar declares Zoom In/Zoom Out buttons with only tooltips/icons. See LogicFriday1/Views/MainWindow.axaml lines 109-125 and 204-208.
- Original resource labels for this menu are in Decompiled/strings.tsv at 004710d6-004712ee: &Gates, &Modify Gate Diagram, &Copy to Clipboard, IC &Package Info..., &Trace Gate Logic, &Auto Redraw Diagram, Zoom &Out, &Zoom All, &Delete Selected, and &Submit Enter. The matching earlier &Cancel Esc string is reused for the Cancel item.
- Gates > Modify Gate Diagram maps to main-window command 0x4b1. It is enabled only when exactly one function is selected, that function has a generated/stored gate diagram, the app is not busy, and no edit mode is active. On invoke, the main command handler sets DAT_00452ec8 = 1 and sends command 0xcd to enter the diagram editor for the selected function. Command 0xcd creates the DiagInWindow, shows GATEPICKDLG2, binds variable/gate-label dialogs, and, when DAT_00452ec8 is set, sends custom command 0x8010 to the editor to load the existing diagram. References: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c lines around command 0x4b1 and case 0xcd; Decompiled/FunctionsTracking.csv entries for FUN_0040cabd and FUN_0042b22e.
- Gates > Copy to Clipboard maps to command 0x151. In normal gate-output view it requires exactly one selected function with a gate diagram and calls FUN_0041509f to copy the current gate diagram to the clipboard as enhanced metafile plus bitmap. In active diagram-edit mode it calls FUN_004151da for the editor/generated metafile view. References: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c around command 0x151; Decompiled/FunctionsMapping.md entries for FUN_0041509f and FUN_004151da.
- Gates > IC Package Info... maps to command 0x156. It requires exactly one selected function with a gate diagram and opens modal dialog ICPKGINFO through FUN_0040aebc. FUN_0040aebc delegates to FUN_0042424d, which displays the precomputed package-count buffer populated by FUN_00423a22. References: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c around command 0x156; Decompiled/FunctionsTracking.csv entry for FUN_0040aebc.
- Gates > Trace Gate Logic maps to command 0x152 and behaves as a checked toggle. When off, invoking it creates modeless TRACEDLG16 via FUN_0040ae7a, checks the menu item, and sends DiagOutWindow command 0x8013 to enable trace display. When on, invoking it unchecks the item, sends command 0x8014 to clear trace state/rebuild the EMF, and destroys the trace dialog. It requires exactly one selected function, a gate diagram, no active edit mode, and not busy. References: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c around command 0x152; Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00437b1f_FUN_00437b1f.c commands 0x8013 and 0x8014; Decompiled/FunctionsTracking.csv entries for FUN_0040ae7a and FUN_00436c32.
- Gates > Auto Redraw Diagram maps to command 0x15a. It requires exactly one selected function with a gate diagram that can be regenerated, no active edit mode, and not busy. On invoke it calls FUN_00434e03 to prepare/regenerate the mapped diagram layout, sets a dirty/regenerated flag when applicable, then refreshes the display with command 0x801d. Reference: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c around command 0x15a; Decompiled/FunctionsTracking.csv entry for FUN_00434e03.
- Gates > Zoom In maps to command 0x153. MainWindow forwards this command to the DiagOutWindow. DiagOutWindow expands the current diagram viewport around the client-area center, sets manual-zoom state, redraws, persists viewport bounds, posts WM_SIZE, and refreshes toolbar/menu state. Enabled when a gate-output diagram is visible, no edit/busy mode is active, and DiagOutWindow query command 0x8016 reports zoom-in is possible in the low word. References: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c menu enable and command forwarding for 0x153; Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00437b1f_FUN_00437b1f.c command 0x153 and query 0x8016.
- Gates > Zoom Out maps to command 0x154. MainWindow forwards it to the DiagOutWindow. DiagOutWindow shrinks/expands the viewport toward fitting the diagram, clamps to client bounds, and if the result fits entirely posts Zoom All command 0x155. Enabled when a gate-output diagram is visible, no edit/busy mode is active, and query 0x8016 reports zoom-out/fitting is needed in the high word. References: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c menu enable and command forwarding for 0x154; Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00437b1f_FUN_00437b1f.c command 0x154 and query 0x8016.
- Gates > Zoom All maps to command 0x155. MainWindow forwards it to the DiagOutWindow. DiagOutWindow computes the fit scale from the diagram extents and client size, resets viewport origin to 0, clears manual-zoom state, redraws, persists viewport bounds, posts WM_SIZE, and refreshes command state. Enabled under the same gate-output/no edit/no busy conditions as Zoom Out when query 0x8016 reports the diagram is not already fully fit. Reference: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/00437b1f_FUN_00437b1f.c command 0x155.
- Gates > Delete Selected maps to command 0x4b2 during active diagram editing. The main handler sends VK_DELETE (0x2e) to DAT_00452a98/DiagInWindow. FUN_0042b22e handles VK_DELETE by calling FUN_004306d3, which deletes selected gates and selected wire segments, repairs connectivity, redraws, invalidates, and updates the window. Enabled only in diagram-edit mode when deletion is available. References: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c command 0x4b2; Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0042b22e_FUN_0042b22e.c VK_DELETE handling; Decompiled/FunctionsTracking.csv entry for FUN_004306d3.
- Gates > Submit (Enter) maps to command 0x114 in diagram-edit mode, which forwards VK_RETURN (0x0d) to DiagInWindow. FUN_0042b22e handles VK_RETURN by validating/compiling the entered gate diagram with FUN_00434112; on success it updates diagram/package metadata, posts main command 0x800f to finish/commit the edit mode, and on validation failure shows either generic errors or FUN_00435ac0 diagram errors. References: Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/004016d2_FUN_004016d2.c command 0x114; Decompiled/logicfriday_decompiled_functions/0042b22e_FUN_0042b22e.c VK_RETURN handling; Decompiled/FunctionsTracking.csv entries for FUN_00434112 and FUN_00435ac0.
